steps.command: echo '# Gotcha Inventory' > artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md && for dir in ~/.claude/rules ~/Projects/agent-infra/.claude/rules ~/Projects/intel/.claude/rules ~/Projects/phenome/.claude/rules ~/Projects/genomics/.claude/rules; do [ -d "$dir" ] && echo "## $dir" >> artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md && grep -l -i 'gotcha\|workaround\|bug\|don.t\|never\|blocked\|broken\|CAPTCHA\|fails\|doesn.t work' "$dir"/*.md 2>/dev/null >> artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md; done && for dir in ~/.claude/projects/*/memory; do [ -d "$dir" ] && echo "## $dir" >> artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md && grep -rl 'gotcha\|workaround\|bug\|broken' "$dir"/*.md 2>/dev/null >> artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md; done && mkdir -p artifacts/gotcha-review

steps.prompt: Read artifacts/gotcha-review/inventory-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md for the list of files containing gotcha-type content. For each file, read it and classify each gotcha/workaround item as: still-relevant | stale (vendor changed, version outdated) | graduated-to-hook (already enforced by a hook) | should-be-code-fix (fix in the code, not a rule). For vendor-specific items, check current versions. Output a structured report to artifacts/gotcha-review/audit-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md with columns: File | Item | Classification | Recommended Action | Confidence. Flag items with 0 fires in 60 days as archive candidates.
